tasks.prompt: Create a comprehensive README.md for claude-swarm. Read all source files in src/ to understand the full feature set. Include: project description, installation (npm install -g), quick start examples (interactive mode, task file, inline tasks), configuration options (models, concurrency, worktrees), architecture overview (planner -> swarm -> agents -> merge), how auth works (OAuth via Claude Agent SDK, works with Max/Pro/API key). Keep it concise and practical — mostly code examples, minimal prose.

Add resilience to src/swarm.ts: 1) Add a configurable per-agent timeout (default 5 minutes) — if an agent exceeds it, abort and mark as error. 2) Add retry logic — if an agent fails with a transient error (rate limit, server error), retry up to 2 times with exponential backoff. 3) Ensure one failing agent never blocks others — the worker loop must continue processing the queue even if one agent throws. Read the current code first to understand the structure.

Add input validation to src/index.ts: 1) Validate task file JSON against expected shape — check 'tasks' is an array, each task has 'prompt' string, reject unknown top-level keys with helpful errors. 2) Validate concurrency is a positive integer. 3) If the cwd doesn't exist, error early with a clear message. 4) If worktrees are requested but we're not in a git repo, error early. Read the current code first.

Create a CONTRIBUTING.md for claude-swarm. Read package.json and src/ to understand the dev setup. Include: prerequisites (Node 20+, Claude Code CLI), development setup (clone, npm install, npm run dev), project structure explanation (src/index.ts entry, swarm.ts core, planner.ts coordinator, ui.ts terminal rendering, types.ts), how to test locally, how to submit PRs. Keep it short and developer-friendly.
